name: Lemon Gelato
recipeInstructions:
1. Grate the zest of 6 lemons and set aside in a bowl with the sugar.
Alternatively, use a vegetable peeler to remove 3/4 cup zest from the lemons and process the lemon strips with the sugar in a food processor until the zest is minced (about 30 seconds). Set the lemons aside.
2. Whisk the egg yolks with the cream and salt in a heatproof mixing bowl.
3. Place the half-and-half and lemon-sugar mixture in a medium saucepan that is non-aluminum. Bring the liquid to a simmer, stirring to dissolve the sugar.
4. Remove the pan from heat. Slowly whisk the hot half-and-half into the egg yolk mixture. Return the partially cooked custard to the saucepan.
5. Stir constantly over medium-low heat until the mixture thickens slightly and coats the back of a spoon (the approximate consistency of unwhipped heavy cream), about 5 minutes.
6. Pour the hot custard into a bowl without straining. Stir in the vanilla extract.
7. Cover the surface of the custard with plastic wrap to prevent skin from forming. Set the custard aside at room temperature to cool.
8. (Optional) Refrigerate the cooled custard for up to 48 hours before freezing.
9. Squeeze 3/4 cup juice from the lemons and stir it into the cooled custard.
10. Transfer the custard mixture to an ice cream maker and freeze according to the manufacturer's instructions.
